mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the high unemployment threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales and housing starts indicate a broad consumer demand contraction, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings growth, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observations within the specified recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 0.3 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Elevated CPIAUCSL readings coincide with positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, breaking the tightening penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting sustained positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly GDP growth reflects strong aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, directly translating to positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day returns, with a point-biserial correlation below 0.01 over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and margin compression, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO trends in late-cycle regimes fail to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 55% of triggered instances.
